mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% within the horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue visibility and equity risk appetite, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with forward returns across three consecutive rolling quarters, indicating decoupling from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production relative to demand constraints signals improved supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, leading to higher earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, demonstrating that supply shocks do not translate to equity outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price levels exceeding the 250 index threshold trigger aggressive Federal Reserve tightening expectations, which compress equity valuations and increase discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain stable despite CPIAUCSL crossing above 250, indicating that inflation thresholds no longer dictate monetary policy expectations or valuation multiples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate cash flows, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year period, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits act as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and financial sector health, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: HOUST growth exhibits a persistent negative correlation with forward equity returns across multiple market cycles, proving it is a lagging rather than leading indicator for S&P 500 performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals weakening credit conditions and consumer confidence, prompting risk-off positioning and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below 1200, indicating that housing data has lost its informational content for equity market direction.
